title: raoulia glabra
intro: Raoulia glabra, also known as the "Copper Cups" or "Pig's Ear," is a succulent plant native to New Zealand. It is a member of the daisy family and is prized for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Raoulia glabra include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and providing antioxidant protection. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, and it is often used in traditional medicine to treat various skin conditions and promote overall well-being.
